title: Tecoma princei
intro: Tecoma princei, commonly known as the Yellow Trumpetbush, is a shrub-like plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Bignoniaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tecoma princei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tecoma princei include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Tecoma princei is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. It is native to Brazil and is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Tecoma princei is a member of the genus Tecoma, which includes various species of trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant is also known as the 'Yellow Trumpetbush' due to its bright yellow flowers. Tecoma princei is often grown as an ornamental plant in gardens and landscapes due to its vibrant flowers and adaptable growth habits.
